description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, alternating between raised stone and recessed black channels. The sides of the block are smooth and solid, with a consistent dark coloration, contrasting with the lighter, marbled stone of the top. The block appears robust and heavy, likely made from a dense stone such as granite or concrete. Its design suggests it could serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, possibly for controlling pedestrian or vehicular movement. The overall finish is utilitarian, with no decorative elements or engravings visible.
description: A heavy, rectangular stone block with a grooved, striped top surface and solid, dark sides. The grooves create a striking contrast and suggest a functional, urban use.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full width of the block with horizontal grooves visible across the top surface.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the depth and spacing of the grooves, showing the striped pattern running from front to back.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, displaying the same horizontal grooves and solid side structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view again emphasizes the deep, parallel grooves and the block's thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears clean with minor marbling typical of stone.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
